channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Bezos, Musk rivalry heats up ahead of Trump’s second term
item.description: A social media spat between Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos this week underscored a sharpening rivalry between two of the world’s richest men at a time when both are trying to grow their sprawling business empires in President-elect Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: The lingering questions around Nvidia’s strong quarterly report
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Technology Letter and is republished here with permission. Late Wednesday evening, some men were sitting around the Russian baths in Wall Street’s financial district talking about Nvidia.
item.dc:creator: Tiernan Ray, Fast Company

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta, stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The future according to Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis
item.description: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis used to get some of his most fulfilling work done between midnight and 3 a.m. A self-described “nocturnal person,” the Londoner devoted the solitude of his overnight shift to catching up on scientific papers, coming up with new ideas, and just plain thinking.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Apple readies more conversational Siri in bid to catch up in AI
item.description: Apple has been testing the upgraded software on iPhones, iPads and Macs as a separate app, but the technology will ultimately replace the Siri interface that users rely on today.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: What is a data architect? Skills, salaries, and how to become a data framework master
item.description: Data architect role Data architects are senior visionaries who translate business requirements into technology requirements, and define data standards and principles, often in support of data or digital transformations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Dell shares its vision of the AI factory powered by NVIDIA
item.description: Factories have been the bedrock of many industries from manufacturing to automotive. But just as factories have fueled the industrial revolution, a new structure will be powering a new transformation in the age of AI: AI factories.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New Windows 11 tool can fix devices that won’t boot remotely
item.description: Microsoft is working on a new Windows feature, “Quick Machine Recovery,” that will allow IT administrators to use Windows Update with “targeted fixes” to remotely fix systems that can’t boot, according to Bleeping Computer.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to protect your deliveries from getting stolen by porch pirates
item.description: Porch pirates are on the prowl this holiday season. Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson has a number of tips to prevent holiday package thefts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cisco: Pressure to deploy AI is up, but only 13% feel ready
item.description: Pressure to implement AI plans is on the rise, but the readiness of enterprise networks to handle AI workloads has actually declined over the past year, according to new research from Cisco.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NetOps startup Selector AI wants to solve the network noise problem
item.description: Back in 2019, Kannan Kothandaraman helped to start Selector AI after spending years in the networking trenches at big vendors including Juniper and Cisco.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cato Networks adds Safe TLS inspection capabilities to SASE platform
item.description: Secure access service edge provider Cato Networks today announced it added safe transport layer security inspection capabilities to its SASE platform to enable customers to inspect TLS traffic more easily and quickly.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft rebrands Azure AI Studio to Azure AI Foundry
item.description: Microsoft is packaging its Azure AI Studio and other updates into a new service — Azure AI Foundry in response to enterprises’ need to develop, run, and manage generative AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Akka distributed computing platform adds Java SDK
item.description: Akka, formerly known as Lightbend, has released Akka 3, an update to the JVM-based distributed computing platform that adds a Java SDK as well as serverless and “bring your own cloud” deployment options. Akka 3 and the company’s name change were announced November 15.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Library of Congress says an adversary hacked some emails
item.description: The library said that an adversary accessed email communications during the period from January to September.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation
